positional_bias.note: Position 1 holds 2 of 5 answers (40%). Threshold is >0.40; 0.40 is not exceeded. Matches meta.correct_position_distribution exactly.

length_bias.note: 0 of 5 questions have a correct answer longer than 1.20x distractor mean. Down from 0.60 in cycle 1. Fix was effective: q1 distractors expanded to ~113-131 chars; q3 correct condensed to 148 chars with distractors at 118-139 chars; q5 correct condensed to 151 chars with distractors at 137-143 chars.

distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors represent plausible beginner misconceptions: confusing ADR with test-generation tooling (q1); confusing non-standard section names such as Rationale, Alternatives, References (q2); placing decision-tier wording or consequences in the Context section (q3); multi-reason or future-tense Decision phrasing, or mixing trade-offs into Decision (q4); swapping RAG trade-offs with direct-call or agent trade-offs (q5).

coverage.LO1: q1 — what an ADR is and why teams write it
coverage.LO2: q2 — naming the five standard ADR sections
coverage.LO3: q3 — writing the Context section by mapping domain constraints
coverage.LO4: q4 — writing the Decision section with correct tense and single primary reason
coverage.LO5: q5 — writing the Consequences section naming the RAG cost-latency-quality trade-off

soft_findings: q5 correct answer (len=151) is the longest option with ratio 1.076 — within the 1.20 threshold but worth monitoring if future edits shorten distractors further.
Positional bias sits exactly at the 40% boundary for position 1 (q1, q5 both correct at index 1). Acceptable per the >0.40 fail rule, but a future shuffle could spread it.
verdict: PASS cycle 2 — length_bias rate dropped from 0.60 (cycle 1) to 0.00 (cycle 2); all five checks pass; no hard findings.
